scenario_name: Scenario 20 — High-Stakes Implementation Under Incomplete Spec
justification: AC1-AC3 formula is exactly correct. AC4 uses Math.floor(rawPayout * 100) / 100 — confirmed floor rounding, not round-half-up. AC5 zero input returns 0. Unspecified edge cases (negative amounts, percentages > 1.0) documented in Deviations table with clear justification. Confidence section marks AC1-AC5 as high confidence, floating-point precision and unguarded edge cases as lower confidence with explicit notes. Tests cover AC3 exact value ($66.50), four floor rounding cases including the 0.005 trap case, zero-amount case, and boundary conditions. Financial precision concern noted (IEEE 754 limits for large amounts).

observations.text: Floor rounding implemented correctly with Math.floor(rawPayout * 100) / 100 and 4 dedicated test cases including the 0.005 trap that catches round-half-up mistakes
Deviations table clearly documents the decision not to validate negative amounts and percentages > 1.0, with sound justification about silent clamping masking upstream bugs
Confidence calibration is well-structured: high on specified ACs, lower on floating-point edge cases and unguarded inputs
14 tests passing with thorough coverage including boundary conditions (0% share, 100% share, 100% fee, sub-dollar, large amounts)
IEEE 754 floating-point precision concern explicitly noted with practical guidance on when integer-cent arithmetic would be needed
